Join a class
Tuesday Evening · Weekly
General
Movement
The main weekly class. Strength, coordination, partner practice, rhythm, problem-solving, and physical challenge. You do not need to be advanced. You do need to be willing to try, fail, adjust, and keep practicing.
All levels Denver Ongoing
Reserve a Spot →
Monday and Thursday Morning · June–July
Sprint &
Athletics
Speed, power, elastic strength, conditioning, and the athletic foundations of movement practice. Small group training built around sprinting, breathing, recovery, and repeated exposure.
Early Morning Outdoors Ongoing
See the Tracks →
The practice
Failure
is part
of the map.

We practice to get stronger, more coordinated, more mobile, more expressive, more capable. Then the work deepens. You start to notice how you meet difficulty, how you learn, how you recover, how you relate to other people — and how much of practice is simply the willingness to return to that space.

Most people here are wanting to create something. A life, a feeling, a purpose, a connection, a depth. This is a practice ground for agency, perception and action.

This movement school grew out of the closure of ApeCo Denver, which is where most of our practitioners started with their movement practice.

As a teacher my own education is not finished. I am a student first, passing along my process and creating containers to help others expand. I've been a therapeutic bodyworker for seven years; I practice the art of healing touch and presence. I see athletes, parents, college students, the elderly. All ages, all backgrounds. Denver Movement School is for any age and capability; only the willingness to learn and develop is required.

Coordination begins with disorientation. Strength comes through stressing the tissue, failure reps and recovery. Skill grows through repeated contact with the edge of your current ability. Movement practice is a vehicle for your development, interconnected through the fabric of the rest of your life.

All levels are welcome. Nothing is forced. Everything can be scaled.
What we practice — Movimentica
01
Fighting
Striking, grappling, contact, timing, and the honest conversation between two bodies.
02
Dance
Open forms, floorwork, improvisation, rhythm, and expressive movement.
03
Athletics
Running, jumping, climbing, balancing, rolling, and adapting to the environment.
04
Preparedness
Strength, mobility, breath, sprinting, conditioning, and usable capacity.
05
Acrobatics
Ground acrobatics and movement, inversion, falling, rolling, and learning at the edge.
06
Inner Work
Attention, patience, fear, failure, feedback, and the ability to keep returning.
CYCLE
Enrolling for Fall
Full
Practice
Cycle
A structured training block across all six pillars — strength, rhythm, athletics, partnering, acrobatics, and inner work. Four sessions a week, eight weeks, a small cohort of people serious about developing a real practice. This is the school in full form.

Coming This Summer

Movement
Day
A free community gathering for people who want to move, meet the school, and feel the practice directly. Multiple teachers, multiple disciplines, one afternoon of shared training. Open to all levels. Date to be announced.

Start
where
you are.
Everything can be adjusted to your level. You can stop, step back, observe, or try again. You will not be forced. You will be encouraged to pay attention, practice sincerely, and keep returning.

— Misha Lantsov
Founder, Denver Movement School
